meta.title: LLM Reasoning Topology: From Map and Path to Terrain Modification
meta.short_tagline: A structural taxonomy of LLM reasoning evolving from static latent maps to dynamic, test-time terrain modification.

overview.title: Overview
overview.intro: Current Large Language Model architectures are evolving across three distinct reasoning regimes. Moving beyond the dichotomy of a static 'Map' (latent space) and a sequential 'Path' (Chain of Thought), the frontier now includes dynamic terrain modification during inference.

summary_card.label: The Three-Act Architecture
summary_card.title: Navigating and Reshaping the Latent Topology
summary_card.body: LLMs have traditionally relied on a frozen latent space (System 1) and linear token generation (System 2). New paradigms introduce test-time gradient descent (System 3), allowing the model to reshape its internal landscape to bridge topological gaps and execute continuous, multi-dimensional search.

key_concepts.title: Key Concepts

cards.title: The Linguistic Bottleneck
cards.body: Sequential token generation collapses high-dimensional continuous thought into a one-dimensional stream. Forcing rich reasoning into linear tokens destroys structural information present in the latent manifold.

cards.title: The Binding Problem & Directional Asymmetry
cards.body: Transformers lack a bidirectional binding register. Causal language modeling creates asymmetric gradient paths, making relationships one-way and causing the Reversal Curse (e.g., knowing A causes B, but failing to infer B is caused by A).

cards.title: Continuous Latent Thought (BFS vs DFS)
cards.body: Methods like COCONUT feed hidden states directly back as inputs without decoding into tokens. This enables a breadth-first search (BFS) capability across multiple reasoning branches, overcoming the depth-first search (DFS) limits of standard CoT.

cards.title: System 3: Terrain Modification
cards.body: Instead of just finding a path through a frozen map, emerging models use test-time gradient descent in latent space to dynamically reshape their local manifold geometry during a query, actively writing new map contour lines while navigating.

key_concepts.intro: Core architectural paradigms driving the evolution of LLM inference and reasoning capabilities.

workflow.title: Evolution of Inference Trajectories

steps.label: System 0/1: Token Probability Lookup
steps.summary: Direct generation via frozen weights and single forward passes.
bullets: Relies entirely on the pre-trained latent map.
Static traversal with no active path planning.
Highly vulnerable to topological gaps and the Reversal Curse.

steps.label: System 1.5: Token-Space CoT
steps.summary: Sequential reasoning token generation using an inference-time token budget.
bullets: Creates an explicit, linear path across the static map.
Forces depth-first search (DFS).
Subject to the linguistic bottleneck.

steps.label: System 2: Latent Continuous Reasoning
steps.summary: Continuous hidden-state recurrence without tokenizing intermediate steps.
bullets: Operates entirely in latent space.
Enables multi-dimensional tree traversal and implicit BFS.
Defers early commitment to specific words until final output.

steps.label: System 3: Dynamic Terrain Modification
steps.summary: Test-time gradient descent locally rewrites the map geometry.
bullets: Perturbs latent weights dynamically at query time.
Path reshapes local topology, bridging fundamental structural voids.
Blurs the rigid boundary between training and inference.

workflow.intro: How LLMs navigate from query to answer across progressing generations of cognitive architecture.

checklist.title: Diagnostic Checklist for Reasoning Failures

items.label: Assess the Reversal Curse
bullets: Is the relationship bidirectional in the prompt?
Does the model fail when querying from object to subject?
If yes, this indicates a directed weight asymmetry in the latent map.

items.label: Identify Linguistic Bottlenecks
bullets: Does forcing Chain of Thought degrade the nuance of the concept?
Is the model losing high-dimensional context when outputting tokens?
Consider testing latent recurrence methods to preserve dimensional richness.

items.label: Evaluate Structural Voids
bullets: Are hallucinations plausible-sounding but ungrounded in fact?
Is the model confabulating paths that simply do not exist in the training manifold?
Consider if the underlying binding or mapping is fundamentally missing.

checklist.intro: Criteria to evaluate whether a model failure is due to weak pathfinding or fundamental topological gaps.

metrics.intro: Quantifiable signals measuring the success of latent pathfinding and terrain modification techniques.

items.name: Latent Search Breadth
items.description: Measures the model's ability to maintain superposition of multiple reasoning branches.
signals: Number of implicit BFS branches successfully sustained
Measurable delay of premature token commitment

items.name: Test-Time Accuracy Delta
items.description: Improvement in reasoning benchmarks achieved via dynamic adaptation without fine-tuning.
signals: Reduction in total model calls by up to 40.2%

items.name: Bidirectional Retrieval Success
items.description: Ability to successfully navigate logically symmetric bindings backward.
signals: Accuracy on B-to-A queries after exclusively training on A-to-B data

metrics.title: Reasoning Efficiency & Efficacy Metrics

personas.intro: A comparative view of reasoning regimes based on their interaction with the latent map.

cells: Explicit DFS path on static Map
Path dynamically reshapes local Map topology

personas.title: Architectural Regimes

failure_modes.name: The Reversal Curse
failure_modes.description: Models cannot reason backward from target to source due to directional gradient flows embedded during autoregressive training.
mitigations: Implement bidirectional diffusion architectures or binding registers.
Utilize dynamic terrain modification (System 3) to build new relational bridges at test time.

failure_modes.name: Linguistic Compression Loss
failure_modes.description: Information is irrevocably lost when continuous latent vectors are projected into discrete vocabulary tokens during CoT.
mitigations: Deploy continuous latent thought recurrences (e.g., COCONUT).
Iterate hidden states deeply before generating surface tokens to maintain dimensionality.

failure_modes.name: Implicit Pruning in BFS
failure_modes.description: Even in latent reasoning models, softmax normalization and learned priors can cause premature suppression of valid reasoning paths.
mitigations: Calibrate reward signals to explicitly maintain search tree diversity during inference.

resilience.intro: Known vulnerabilities in navigating latent topologies and emerging methods to mitigate them.
resilience.title: Failure Modes & Mitigations

structure.intro: How abstract components of cognitive navigation correspond to specific neural operations in modern LLMs.

cells: Static associative landscape of interconnected concepts.
Linear step-by-step traversal along predefined gradients.
Parallel BFS-biased reasoning deferring token collapse.
Reshaping manifold geometry locally during active queries.

structure.title: Anatomy of the Three-Act Architecture
